care.summary: Climbing perennial with feathery, fern-like foliage; not a true fern but often grown as one indoors.
care.light: Bright indirect light; tolerates medium light
care.water: Keep evenly moist; do not let dry out completely
care.humidity: Medium to high; mist regularly in dry rooms
care.fertilizer: Balanced liquid feed every 4 weeks during growing season
care.toxicity: Toxic to cats and dogs if ingested; sap may irritate skin
common_issues: Yellowing fronds from overwatering or low humidity
Spider mites in dry indoor air
tips: Provide a small trellis for climbing habit
Mist fronds frequently to deter spider mites
